reasoning: The question asks whether Rwanda will experience at least 11 ACLED-recorded 'Battles' in any 30-day period before four resolution dates in 2025. Over the 360 days preceding May 2025, the baseline average effectively recorded only 1 battle in Rwanda for the entire year. This period included the massive early-2025 M23 offensive and the fall of Goma right on the Rwanda-DRC border. Because that major escalation did not lead to more than 1 battle being coded *in Rwanda*, future spillover is extremely unlikely to cross the 11-battle threshold in a single month. Recent intelligence suggests that the DRC and M23 are actively pursuing direct peace talks, reducing the near-term risk of a new border flare-up. Furthermore, domestic political conditions in Rwanda (under President Kagame) are completely stable with no ongoing insurgency capable of generating regular intra-state combat. Consequently, the probability of 11 distinct battles inside Rwandan borders over 30 days is exceptionally low for all listed dates. I maintain a conservative 5% base minimum due to limits, slightly increasing with time to account for unforeseen black swan events (e.g., peace talks completely collapsing into a larger regional war).
